title: Share of people who have depressive disorders, by age
subtitle: Estimated share of people who have  [depressive disorders](#dod:depressive_disorders), whether or not they are diagnosed, based on representative surveys, medical data and statistical modelling.
caption: In 2023, the estimated share of people with depressive disorders varies significantly across age groups, ranging from under 1% to over 5%. The horizontal bar chart displays these percentages, with the lowest prevalence recorded for children aged 5-14 at 0.98%. The share rises to 4.55% for the 15-19 age group and continues to increase through early adulthood, reaching 4.91% for ages 25-29. The highest prevalence occurs among older adults, with the 65-69 age group showing the peak value at 5.46%, followed closely by those aged 60-64 at 5.4% and ages 70+ at 5.36%. The age-standardized estimate sits at 3.81%, while the figure for all ages combined is 4%. The distribution shows a distinct pattern where prevalence is lowest in childhood, rises through young adulthood, and remains elevated among the elderly population.
